# datahub_glossary_term (Data Source)

**DataHub ✅ | DataHub Cloud ✅**

Looks up an existing DataHub glossary term by `term_id`.

Use this to reference a term that already exists in DataHub -- for example one created in the DataHub UI or via the Python SDK -- as a `parent_node` input or to read its URN for use in other resources (e.g. policies).

## Example Usage

```terraform
data "datahub_glossary_term" "revenue" {
  term_id = "revenue"
}

output "revenue_urn" {
  value = data.datahub_glossary_term.revenue.urn
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `term_id` (String) The unique identifier of the glossary term to look up (the URN suffix, e.g. `revenue`).

### Read-Only

- `description` (String) Definition of the term. Empty if not set.
- `name` (String) Human-readable display name of the term.
- `parent_node` (String) Full URN of the parent glossary node (Term Group), or empty if this term is at the root level.
- `urn` (String) Full DataHub URN for this glossary term (e.g. `urn:li:glossaryTerm:revenue`).
